channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NAFDAC Confirms BON Bread Meets All Safety and Regulatory Requirements After Investigation
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) don carry out proper investigation on the matter wey people dey talk about BON Bread wey some person claim say e no dey get mo...

item.title: Study Suggests Infrasound May Cause 'Haunted' Feelings in Homes
item.description: A team of Canadian scientists, led by Dr. Rodney Schmaltz of MacEwan University in Edmonton, has investigated why some people feel uneasy in old or supposedly haunted buildings. Their research points ...

item.title: Dr. Hook frontman Dennis Locorriere passes away at 76 following long kidney disease battle
item.description: Dennis Locorriere, the frontman of the 1970s soft rock band Dr. Hook, has died at the age of 76 after a long and courageous battle with kidney disease. He passed away peacefully on 16th May 2026, surr...

item.title: Trump's credibility issues could influence 2026 midterm elections, attorney says
item.description: Attorney and columnist Alan Scott Bolden has highlighted potential political consequences stemming from President Donald Trump's credibility issues. Bolden argues that Trump's history of false or misl...

item.title: OPP charges several Muskoka drivers in crackdown on impaired and dangerous driving
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) recently conducted a focused enforcement operation in the Muskoka region targeting impaired and dangerous driving. During this blitz, multiple drivers were arrested and...

item.title: Perth surveyor Steven Mattaboni dies in shark attack at Rottnest Island
item.description: Steven Mattaboni, a 38-year-old surveyor from Perth, tragically lost his life in a shark attack at Horseshoe Reef near Rottnest Island on Saturday morning. An experienced fisherman and devoted father,...

item.title: Javier Bardem claims Hollywood is shifting away from blacklisting actors who support Palestine
item.description: Oscar-winning actor Javier Bardem has stated that Hollywood is undergoing a significant change regarding actors who publicly support Palestine. Speaking at the Cannes Film Festival while promoting his...

item.title: Motorway Authorities Restrict Vehicles Carrying Sacrificial Animals Ahead of Eidul Azha for Safety
item.description: Islamabad ki National Highway and Motorway Police (NHMP) nay announcement kiya hai ke Eidul Azha se pehlay, motorways par janwar le jaane wali gaadiyon ka entry banned hai. Official statement ke mutab...

item.title: Everyday Life and Culture in Northern Argentina During the 1930s
item.description: This article explores life in Northern Argentina during the 1930s, contrasting it with the European-style elegance of Buenos Aires. In provinces such as Salta, Jujuy, and Tucumán, daily life revolved ...

item.title: Gauteng Schools Face R584m Municipal Debt Amid Billing Disputes
item.description: As of March 2026, Gauteng schools owe municipalities a total of nearly R584 million for electricity, water usage, and property rates, according to the province's MEC for Education, Lebogang Maile. The...

item.title: Sundowns Take Narrow 1-0 Advantage Over AS FAR in Caf Champions League Final First Leg
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns managed to secure a 1-0 victory over AS FAR in the first leg of the 2025-26 Caf Champions League final at Loftus on Sunday. The only goal came in the 37th minute when defender Aubrey...

item.title: Mosaic outlines 2030 plan to expand employment opportunities for Indigenous communities in Regina
item.description: Mosaic, a leading Canadian company with operations in Regina, has introduced a comprehensive 2030 action plan aimed at increasing employment opportunities for Indigenous peoples. The plan outlines str...

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Final Week on 'The Late Show' to Feature Iconic Guests Including Springsteen and Spielberg
item.description: Stephen Colbert is set to bid farewell to 'The Late Show' with a star-studded final week. After announcing the show’s cancellation in July last year, which came as a shock to the television industry, ...

item.title: Songbayanang Choir impresses with perfect performance on Everybody Sing
item.description: The Songbayanang Choir recently captivated audiences with a flawless rendition of Jason Dy's popular song 'Minsan Lang Kita Iibigin' on the hit Philippine game show Everybody Sing. Their performance e...

item.title: Professor Green discusses his experiences with ADHD and autism and offers advice to students
item.description: Stephen Manderson, known professionally as Professor Green, has opened up about his struggles with ADHD and autism during his school years. Diagnosed later in life, he reflected on the difficulties he...

item.title: KZN Premier launches forensic probe into alleged irregular appointments at Enterprise iLembe
item.description: The Office of the Premier in KwaZulu-Natal has launched a forensic investigation into Enterprise iLembe, the economic development agency linked to the iLembe District Municipality, following serious a...

item.title: Meet the Muslim musician who can recite 2,500 Mahabharat couplets and received Padma Shri | I Witness
item.description: This video tells the story of a Muslim musician in India who has memorised and can recite 2,500 couplets from the Mahabharat, one of the great Indian epics. It also highlights his recognition by the I...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard reports Chinese vessel conducting unpermitted marine research at Pag-asa Cays
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) reported that a Chinese research vessel, Xiang Yang Hong 33, was conducting unauthorized marine scientific research near Cay-2 and Cay-3 close to Pag-asa Island in the...

item.title: Kenya Kwanza Leaders Contribute Millions to Churches in Kilifi and Kitui to Support Development
item.description: Kenya Kwanza government officials have recently donated millions of shillings to churches in Kilifi and Kitui counties to support various development initiatives. CS Hassan Joho gave KSh 1 million to ...

item.title: Punjab Government Expands Hypertension Screening, Treatment and Prevention Programmes
item.description: On World Hypertension Day, Punjab’s healthcare initiatives are taking significant strides in combating high blood pressure, commonly known as the 'silent killer'. The Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojna (MMSY) is...
